entry.title: Washington Post: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Israel and Egypt sign gas export deal as Europe seeks Russia alternative
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Surrender deadline passes in Severodonetsk; NATO discusses defense aid
  • As prices rise, Europeans divided over how Ukraine war should end
  • U.S., NATO allies meet to discuss more military aid for Ukraine
  • Tokyo moves to allow same-sex partnerships, but not as legal marriage
  • Scottish leader pushes independence vote, is rebuked by Boris Johnson
  • 5 countries hit hard by the grain crisis in Ukraine
  • For Colombia’s candidates, WhatsApp mockery can be good publicity
  • As K-pop behemoth BTS hits pause, its fandom reels
  • Read the work of missing British journalist Dom Phillips
  • Swiss airspace reopens after computer failure grounds flights for hours
  • ‘Zero covid’ has many in China dreaming of leaving
  • The case for and against Biden visiting Saudi Arabia
  • Russia made nearly $100B from fuel exports in war’s first 100 days, report says
  • Casualties rise as Russia makes incremental gains in east, Pentagon official says
  • Ukraine battle intensifies as Western backers mull new military aid
  • Hundreds of little penguins are washing up dead on New Zealand shores
  • U.K. cancels flight to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda after court challenges
  • Ukraine war brings peace — between Canada and Denmark
  • Brazil apologizes for telling journalist’s family bodies were found
  • Israeli court finds Gaza aid worker guilty on terror charges
  • Italy creates new museum for trafficked ancient artifacts
  • Pakistani govt chided for 'Drink less tea, save money' plea
  • Sweden: Cartoonist Vilks' fatal car crash was an accident
  • Blas expected to become hurricane in Pacific
  • Suspect in Buffalo grocery massacre charged with federal hate crimes
  • Microsoft’s Internet Explorer is dead
  • A school made girls wear skirts. A court ruled it unconstitutional.
  • Bronx Zoo’s Happy the elephant is not a legal person, court rules
  • Klay Thompson lookalike banned after fooling security at Warriors’ arena
  • Torn from his first love in the Korean War, strangers just helped them reunite
  • The gun deal could close the ‘boyfriend loophole.’ Here’s what it is.
  • Post Politics Now: Trump notches primary victories; Biden asks oil companies to help with gas prices
  • Primary elections live updates: Tom Rice ousted in S.C. after Trump impeachment vote, and the GOP scores a win in Texas
  • Publix heiress paid Kimberly Guilfoyle’s $60,000 speaking fee on Jan. 6
  • Jan. 6 panel divided over whether to issue criminal referral of Trump
  • Libya oil output strangled from infighting in world starved of energy
  • Fisher-Price, regulators issue warning on baby rockers after 13 deaths
  • Seven ways you can financially prepare for a recession
  • We’re still not in a recession despite the stock market drop
  • Teslas running Autopilot involved in 273 crashes reported since last year
  • ‘TMNT: Shredder’s Revenge’ is a kung fu barrage of arcade perfection
  • Future of Work: Rite Aid wants to put your pharmacist in your pocket
  • Bellevue is getting noticed for all the right reasons
  • Water sensors can help you maintain a dry basement
  • The Boro in Tysons gets expansion approval for residential development
  • Senators Treat Crypto Like a Digital New Jersey Turnpike
  • Long Covid Is Showing Up in the Employment Data
  • In Tuesday’s Primaries, a Big Day for Donald Trump
  • Housing Market Cooldown Will Only Lead to More Dysfunction
  • Why Japan’s Yen Is the Weakest in 20 Years and What That Means
  • Why Closer Ties Between Russia and China Have Democracies Worried
  • The Boom in Private Markets Has Transformed Finance. Here’s How
  • The U.S. must oppose India’s rising Islamophobia
  • Democrats can’t have it both ways about the threat of GOP extremism
  • Can America ‘do big things’ again? Ask the regulators and lawyers.
  • Is AI sentient? Wrong question.
  • Biden’s rousing speech before the AFL-CIO is exactly the message Democrats need
  • Thomas lawsuit takes up House of Delegates election challenge
  • Fairfax County is a leader in promoting red-flag laws. Others should follow.
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Republicans Who Deny 2020 Election Outcome Press Closer to Power Over Future Elections

  • Romania Sees an Opening to Become an Energy Power in Europe

  • What Europe’s Universal Charger Mandate Means for You

entry.title: Financial Times: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ECB to design new bond-buying plan to tackle market turmoil
  • Italian bonds and bank stocks rally as ECB signals help for weaker economies
  • ECB has ‘no limits’ in defence of euro from debt turmoil, says executive
  • Oil vs human rights: Biden’s controversial mission to Saudi Arabia
  • Crypto exchanges slash jobs as market turmoil triggers trading downturn
  • WorldCom’s stark warning for today’s bear market
  • Live news updates: HSBC fires London forex trader after probe into client messaging

  • Macron, Scholz and Draghi expected in Kyiv to support Ukraine

  • Global banking regulator urges closer links between pay and climate risks
  • Gfanz kicks off carbon cutting proposal
  • Harold Hamm: private investors will pay for oil exploration markets shun
  • Pharma bro David Shaw
  • SEC proposal on fund names looms over everything, not just ESG

  • London market’s efforts at reinvigoration are flagging
  • China under pressure to reform debt market as foreign inflows slow
  • BP takes 40% stake in vast $30bn Australian renewables project
  • US government debt falls on expectations of sharper rate rises
  • EU lawmakers rebel against green label for gas and nuclear
  • Greece’s first climate crisis minister prepares for summer heatwave
  • Barbados PM: Climate change requires a new financial architecture for us all
  • Italy’s media is being roiled by rows over Russian propaganda
  • Leaving Horizon would make a mockery of Britain’s desire to be a science superpower
  • Ukraine war forces Swiss business to make choices on neutrality

entry.title: Guardian: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Royal Ascot 2022: State Of Rest wins Prince of Wales’s Stakes on day two – live!
  • Russia-Ukraine war: thousands of civilians trapped in Sievierodonetsk with water supplies dwindling, UN says – live
  • Abortion and gun decisions loom as US supreme court releases more opinions – live
  • No 10 revives prospect of UK leaving European convention on human rights after Labour calls Rwanda plans ‘a shambles’ – live
  • Mélenchon’s lesson to the left: less socialism, more social democracy | Paolo Gerbaudo
  • How cannabis-fed chickens may help cut Thai farmers’ antibiotic use
  • Russia rakes in nearly $20bn from oil exports in May
  • A migrant takes a serene selfie – Adam Ferguson’s best photograph
  • Will Squid Game: The Challenge be the darkest reality show ever?
  • Cryptocurrency ‘bloodbath’ threatens multibillion-dollar hedge fund
  • Elon Musk suggests he may vote for Republican Ron DeSantis in 2024
  • Like Trump’s wall, the Rwanda deportation flight was only ever a metaphor | Marina Hyde
  • WHO to rename monkeypox virus to avoid discrimination
  • Childcare costs discriminate against mothers. Britain has to do better | Laura Bates

  • ‘Deep concern’ over fate of Dom Phillips in Brazil, says Boris Johnson

  • Italian woman admits killing ‘kidnapped’ four-year-old daughter
  • Tensions heighten in Taiwan Strait as China acts to extend military operations
  • Spain offer fresh evidence in attempt to reverse Rugby World Cup ban
  • Hot baths, ice lollies, Vaseline: 10 ways to keep exercising in a heatwave
  • River pushed off course as floods leave Yellowstone ‘dramatically changed’
  • Sealed Back to the Future VHS tape sells for $75,000 at US auction
  • Markets brace for sharpest rise in US interest rates in almost 30 years
  • EU Brexit chief suggests MPs should stop Northern Ireland protocol bill
  • Casey Stoney on coaching San Diego, Pride Month and leaving family behind
  • Bairstow defends decision to play in the IPL instead of County Championship
  • I’ll shout from the rooftops that I use food banks. There should be no shame | Liz
  • Francis Bacon portrait of Lucian Freud expected to sell for over £35m at auction
  • ‘Meaningful change’ comes to London fashion week
  • How to make the perfect chocolate sorbet – recipe | Felicity Cloake's How to make the perfect…
  • Sturgeon plans to hold second Scottish independence referendum in October 2023
  • World Cup 2022 power rankings: breaking down the final 32
  • TechScape: why you shouldn’t worry about sentient AI … yet
  • It’s Labour’s job to lead the fight for worker and migrant rights – why isn’t it doing so? | Owen Jones
  • Parts of John Hughes’ novel The Dogs copied from The Great Gatsby and Anna Karenina
  • Red Sauce Brown Sauce by Felicity Cloake review – a quest for the great British breakfast
  • To infinity – actually, let’s go back: why can film franchises no longer look to the future?
  • Stanley Cup final 2022: five things to know before Lightning v Avalanche
  • New data reveals extraordinary global heating in the Arctic
  • A knight’s tale: Brindley Sherratt on the stamina and storytelling of Wagner’s Parsifal
  • ‘I swear I saw faces in the darkness’: can you scare yourself happy?
  • ‘Landmark’ anthology 100 Queer Poems published for Pride month
  • Paul Pogba reaches verbal agreement to rejoin Juventus on free transfer
  • It took a human rights court to halt No 10’s Rwanda flight – and act with basic humanity | Enver Solomon
  • Ozzy Osbourne ‘doing well and on the road to recovery’ following surgery
  • Grenfell Tower fire: fifth anniversary marked with silent march – in pictures
  • New Hong Kong textbooks ‘will claim city never was a British colony’
  • Trump’s raising of $250m for fund that ‘did not exist’ suggests possible fraud
  • US reeling from tampon shortage as prices of menstrual products go up
  • I took the train to Trieste – here’s my guide
  • A moment that changed me: I watched my mother dance in nipple tassles on TV – and my heart swelled with pride
  • Sri Lanka government workers get Fridays off to grow food ahead of shortages
  • ‘The Beatles? I was more a fan of the Beach Boys’: Peter Blake at 90 on pop art and clubbing with the Fab Four
  • ‘Justice’ for Ukraine overshadowed by cost of living concerns, polling shows
  • Vanuatu, one of the last Covid hermit nations, to open to tourists after two years
  • A brother’s 36-year fight against one of New Zealand’s worst miscarriages of justice
  • England fans finally have a stick to beat Gareth Southgate with | Barney Ronay
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 15 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 15 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-15 14:49PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Yahoo joins Facebook owner Meta in testing metaverse tech in Hong Kong
  • Patients almost 10 times more likely to survive cardiac arrests with CPR or AED applied, but few in Hong Kong receive such aid, research finds
  • Domestic helper died of natural causes, rules Hong Kong inquest jury, despite allegations of overwork, bad working conditions
  • Hong Kong top officials to ‘undergo 3-day hotel quarantine’ ahead of visit by state leaders to mark city’s 25th handover anniversary
  • Migrant workers’ groups slam Hong Kong government for excluding domestic helpers in second batch of consumption vouchers
  • Hong Kong’s Greater Bay Airlines to launch first scheduled commercial flight to Bangkok in July, fleet expansion plans on hold amid Covid border measures
  • Honeymoon years (1997-2002): Hong Kong’s early period under Chinese rule in 25 photos
  • Hong Kong customs arrests 51-year-old woman on suspicion of laundering more than HK$350 million over 3 years
  • Hong Kong ranks 5th in list of world’s most competitive economies, climbing 2 spots over improved performance amid Covid pandemic
  • SCMP picks Catherine So as chief executive, tapping Hong Kong media and tech veteran to run one of Asia’s oldest English newspapers
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong reports over 1,000 cases for first time in more than 2 months as nightspot patrons cancel bookings ahead of new rule launch
  • Hong Kong legislators question transport bureau’s proposal for congestion levy to ease traffic jams at cross-harbour tunnels
  • Hong Kong slips one place to fourth most expensive city for luxury spending as hotel rates take a huge hit from pandemic, Julius Baer report finds
  • Hong Kong judges and barristers could wave goodbye to courtroom wigs in run-up to 2047, says city’s first chief justice
  • Exclusive interview: Hong Kong’s first chief justice Andrew Li on whether all judges in city should be Chinese nationals ahead of 2047
  • Climate Change: China’s firms lag behind Asia, North America, Europe in net-zero targets with only 4 per cent adherence, study finds
  • Historic Hong Kong mansion handed over to pro-Beijing think tank to transform into healthy living centre
  • Alien hunters detect mystery radio signal from direction of Earthlike planet
  • China’s digital currency: e-CNY trials move beyond retail to cover corporate loans for small businesses and tax payments
  • UN human rights chief ‘not able to speak to’ any detained Uygurs or their families in Xinjiang
  • Meet China’s new live-streaming e-commerce star: an English-language tutor who lost his off-campus teaching job
  • Lithuania plans to open Taiwan office in September, says exports to mainland China ‘close to zero’
  • As Taiwan reopens its borders, is it ready to live with Covid?
  • China’s bank runs highlight abuse among small-bank shareholders, despite crackdown
  • China coronavirus: ‘you expire in 3 days’ restrictions and negative test proof requirements hit daily life hard
  • Bar at centre of latest Beijing Covid cluster loses its licence as staff face criminal investigation
  • Ant Group’s payment services platform Alipay is most trusted brand in China, new survey by US firm finds
  • Why archaeologists are so excited by China’s Sanxingdui ruins
  • Taiwan-US security and military talks will discuss weapons and strategies to defend island against Beijing
  • China’s internet watchdog tightens mobile app rules for national security, requires promotion of ‘core socialist values’
  • As China’s economic slowdown moderates, analysts call for greater policy easing: ‘it will be a long haul back’
  • China refrains from policy rate cut, defying market expectations once again
  • How China’s belt and road diplomacy could end the Ukraine conflict for good
  • Veteran Chinese human rights lawyers expected to face trial soon
  • Chubb CEO Evan Greenberg urges US businesses to stay engaged in China market
  • Backlash against Russians living abroad is all too familiar for anxious Chinese expats
  • For China’s elite graduates, career dreams take a back seat to stability in coronavirus-hit job market
  • US drug supply chain alternative to China might run through Canada, Latin America
  • Chinese students’ dream device defeats Japan’s most powerful supercomputer in world contest
  • Dozens of countries call out China at UN over Xinjiang abuse allegations
  • Thailand’s opposition party files no-confidence motion against ‘flawed’ PM, elections just months away
  • Can Joe Biden’s proposed Middle East trip unite fractured region by aligning militaries against Iran?
  • ‘Palm oil mafia’ minister out: Indonesia’s Jokowi dumps Lutfi in cabinet reshuffle
  • Indian forces in Kashmir kill militants suspected of targeted shooting, part of increased counter-insurgency effort
  • Japan, Australia to expand defence ties for regional order amid growing fear of China’s presence
  • Singapore ramps up ‘Wolbachia’ mosquito breeding as dengue crisis escalates
  • Polluted air cuts life expectancy by 2 years, South Asia and India most affected, China improving
  • OnlyFans couple sought in Thailand over ‘unacceptable’ sex videos that were leaked online
  • Indonesia’s Joko Widodo to meet Vladimir Putin in Moscow on June 30: Russian report
  • Asia’s energy crunch turns from bad to worse after fire at US plant
  • Australia-China relations: Canberra’s foreign minister Penny Wong to travel to Solomon Islands amid China security pact concerns
  • Deaf Indian boy trapped in well with snakes and scorpions is rescued after four days
  • Coronavirus: New Zealand to eliminate pre-departure Covid-19 tests for incoming passengers
  • India all but sinks WTO sustainable fishing deal; China among contributors to global overfishing
  • Can Sri Lanka tourism recover from ‘triple whammy’ of terrorism, Covid-19 and economic crisis?
  • BTS announce temporary break, with plans for solo releases before reuniting as group – though label denies it’s a ‘hiatus’
  • WHO will share vaccines to stop monkeypox amid inequity fears
  • Sweltering heat stifles a third of US population, severe storms leave thousands in the dark
  • Joe Biden gambles there’s more to gain by visiting ‘pariah’ Saudi Arabia
  • Ukraine fights on after Russia’s surrender ultimatum, evacuation offer for Sievierodonetsk holdouts
  • Who’s coming to Putin’s ‘Russian Davos’ in St Petersburg?
  • Russia turns to old tanks as it burns through weapons in Ukraine, European officials say
  • In Ukraine-Russia war, echoes of WWI with artillery duels, trenches and shell-blasted landscapes
  • Netflix plans Squid Game reality show with US$4.5 million prize, no fatal consequences
  • Canada and Denmark settle decades-old ‘whiskey war’ over Arctic island
  • UK vows to pursue asylum seeker policy after first deportation flight to Rwanda cancelled
  • Iran preparing for rocket launch, satellite images suggest amid nuclear tensions
  • Jailed Putin critic Alexei Navalny moved to high-security penal colony
  • Happy the elephant is not a person, top New York court rules – she is a ‘magnificent creature’
  • Top Chinese developer Country Garden offers to buy back US$683 million bond in show of financial strength amid sector’s protracted debt woes
  • Chinese government dim sum bonds worth US$1.1 billion snapped up by investors, thanks to the attractive yields on offer
  • China’s semiconductor output in May rebounds as Covid-19 restrictions ease
  • Why rising interest rates are bad news for Hong Kong’s housing market
  • Google debate over sentient AI overshadows more pressing issues like prejudice and exploitation, researchers say
  • Emigrating mainland Chinese and Hongkongers could take combined wealth of US$65 billion with them, consultancy says
  • China’s US$20 trillion asset management industry to embrace more diverse products amid revamp, think tank says
  • Alibaba leads Hong Kong stocks to best gain in a week on China data while markets brace for Fed rate hike
  • US likely to single out Tesla for crashes involving Autopilot after collecting data on automated driving
  • China releases new national climate change strategy to build resilience against global warming by 2035
  • China to test market appetite for debt with biggest offshore bond sale since 2016
  • In Hong Kong’s property market, HK$10 million no longer buys what it did in 1997
  • Developers’ aggressive loan schemes could put financially weak buyers at risk if Hong Kong home prices fall, analysts say
  • Hong Kong malls, retailers tap into AI analytics to get smarter after losing US$16 billion of sales in post-2018 industry slump
  • China’s 618 online shopping festival more muted than previous years as platforms shift focus from sales to service quality
  • As the industrial metaverse grows, the platform must remain open and available
  • Yuan currency most exposed to climate change – could lose half its value in 50 years, Barclays says
  • SoftBank plans additional London listing for Arm initial public offering
  • K-drama review: Sh**ting Stars – breezy celebrity-themed workplace comedy starring Kim Young-dae and Lee Sung-kyung loses its early charm
  • Netflix’s 2022 Iron Chef reboot pits culinary legends against rising talents in quest to be the best in the TV kitchen
  • Hong Kong Disneyland launches new fireworks show featuring lasers, water fountains and projections beamed onto Castle of Magical Dreams
  • Best K-pop albums and singles of 2022 so far, from That That by Psy and BTS’ Suga to Stray Kids’ Oddinary and Dreamcatcher’s Apocalypse
  • Just Remembering movie review: Japanese youth romance starring Sairi Ito and Sosuke Ikematsu sees a relationship develop in reverse
  • From minimalism to maximalist design, Singapore apartment full of vintage furniture, collectibles and art is a delight for the senses
  • The best cart noodles in Hong Kong according to a chef, where he goes for spare ribs rice, and memorable meals with his boss, ‘Demon Chef’ Alvin Leung
  • Airport chaos as surge in flight bookings catches travel trade on the hop, with long queues and cancellations
  • What is amla? The Indian gooseberry hailed as a superfood for its apparent anticancer, anti-diabetes and anti-ageing properties
  • The Black Phone movie review: Ethan Hawke plays a sadistic child murderer in Doctor Strange director’s effective horror thriller
  • Are bleached eyebrows right for you? Expert tips on a viral ’90s beauty trend made popular by Kendall Jenner, Maisie Williams and Lizzo
  • Meet Elon Musk’s controversial father, Errol: the Tesla CEO called his South African dad ‘evil’ and denied rumours of family wealth fuelled by an emerald mine – but are they ready to reconcile?
  • Why luxury hotels are all about mental health in 2022: forget five-star spas and Michelin-starred restaurants, guests now demand therapy sessions and on-site counsellors from a wellness retreat
  • 6 K-pop groups who stayed together after leaving their agencies: Got7, 2PM, Girls’ Generation, Mamamoo, Apink and Highlight all made comebacks with their OG members
  • Meet Kim Se-jeong, Today’s Webtoon star: the K-drama actress debuted as a K-pop idol in IOI before acting in K-dramas like Business Proposal on Netflix – but why is she called ‘Korea’s Emma Stone’?
  • Zhou Rui’s provocative fashion: she’s dressed Blackpink’s Lisa, Madison Beer and other stars within just three years of launching her brand thanks to her style that ‘isn’t limited by gender or size’
  • Meet Riley Keough, Elvis Presley’s granddaughter – she modelled for Dior before acting and directing, and ‘loved’ stepdad Michael Jackson, but why didn’t she act in the Elvis biopic?
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
